item.title: Data-driven problems at CoDA 2020!
item.description: Presented on uncertainties in neural networks and had many good discussions around Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) applications.

item.title: I will be attending USC MLUQ workshop and SIGGRAPH 2019, isA
Back to computing research!
item.description: Started a new research adventure at Sandia National Laboratories.

item.title: VPS: Voronoi piecewise surrogate models for high-dimensional data fitting
item.description: A new method to construct credible global surrogates with local accuracy, without dictating where to sample.

item.title: A new gig: Data Science R&D Manager
item.description: Leading an interdisciplinary IRAD team on data science and behavioral analysis research at Northrop Grumman.

item.title: Robust All-quad Meshing of Domains with Connected Regions
item.description: A new algorithm for all-quad meshing of non-convex domains, with connected regions.

item.title: Map-invariant spectral analysis for the identification of DNA periodicities
item.description: A novel algebraic approach to the DNA periodicity detection problem.

I am a Member of Technical Staff at the Center for Computing Research of Sandia National Laboratories, where I work on computational and data science research problems, as part of the Dakota team. I’m virtually located in the Bay Area, CA. I have previously held R&D and management positions at Northrop Grumman Corporation in Albuquerque NM, the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering of UC Davis in Davis CA, the Computational Visualization Center under Oden Institute for Computational Engineering and Sciences at UT Austin in Austin TX, and Cisco Systems in San Jose CA.
